WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR ANDREWS AVE AND NE 5TH ST IN FORT LAUDERDALE, MOVING SE THROUGH FLAGLER VILLAGE AND RIO VISTA TOWARDS PORT EVERGLADES.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG NW SIDE OF FORT LAUDERDALE-HOLLYWOOD INT'L AIRPORT NEAR I-595 AND MOVED ESE ALONG AIRPORT'S NORTHERN TAXIWAY. DAMAGED FEDEX FACILITY AND A JET BRIDGE BEFORE LIKELY CROSSING PERIMETER ROAD AND US 1 ON WAY INTO SOUTHERN END OF PORT EVERGLADES. IT FLIPPED SEVERAL CARGO CONTAINERS AT THE PORT BEFORE CROSSING THE INTRACOASTAL WATERWAY TO THE BARRIER ISLAND WHERE DR. VON D. MIZELL-EULA JOHNSON STATE PARK IS LOCATED.

UPDATE TO PREVIOUSLY REPORTED TORNADO... THE NWS CONDUCTED A STORM SURVEY AND CAN CONFIRM AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN CORAL SPRINS THIS AFTERNOON. DAMAGE WAS MOSTLY TO TREES AND VEGETATION. THE TOP OF TREES HAD TREE BRANCHES SNAPPED AND BROKEN OFF. PLEASE SEE LATEST PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT FOR MORE DETAILED INFORMATION.

NWS SURVEY, ALONG WITH EYEWITNESS REPORTS, INDICATED THAT TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN MIAMI NEAR INTERSECTION OF SOUTHWEST 8TH STREET AND SOUTHWEST 103RD AVENUE. MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES, MAILBOXES, STREET SIGNS, CANOPIES, AND BASKETBALL HOOPS OCCURRED AS THE TORNADO MOVED EAST-SOUTHEAST ALONG 900 BLOCK OF SOUTHWEST 101ST AVENUE AND SOUTHWEST 100TH COURT, BEFORE THE TORNADO BECAME A WATERSPOUT OVER NORTHWEST CORNER OF A LARGE RETENTION POND AND LIFTED. TIMES ARE ESTIMATED BY RADAR AND WITNESS
